title: Dark silicon budget
intro: The full toolbox is yours: architecture, gate length, oxide, sheet geometry, stacking. The product needs 120 µA of drive at 0.7 V — but the thermal budget allows only half a nanowatt of standby leakage per device, or the chip has to power itself down. Every knob that buys speed leaks somewhere. Co-optimize.
explain: You just fought the power wall. Dennard scaling — shrink everything, keep power density constant — broke down around 2005–2007 because threshold and supply voltage stopped scaling while leakage climbed. The result is 'dark silicon': Esmaeilzadeh et al. (ISCA 2011) projected that beyond half of a chip's transistors must sit powered off at the 8 nm node. Your best design balanced drive against leakage instead of maximizing either — exactly the trade every real logic technology makes.
